content.text: RBAC is in place. Now I'll create all required resources in one manifest.

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-public
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        ports:
        - name: grpc
          containerPort: 26257
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -ecx
        - |
          exec /cockroach/cockroach start \
            --insecure \
            --advertise-host=$(hostname -f) \
            --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 \
            --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257 \
            --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257 \
            --cache=25% \
            --max-sql-memory=25%
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 2
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 5
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF
input.description: Apply CockroachDB headless service, public service, PDB, and StatefulSet

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 36); do
  READY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c "Running" || true)
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) pods Running: $READY/3"
  if [ "$READY" -ge 3 ]; then echo "All 3 pods Running"; break; fi
  sleep 5
done

content.thinking:
Ah, I see the problem. StatefulSet creates pods sequentially - it waits for pod-0 to be Ready before creating pod-1. Pod-0 is Running but not Ready (readiness probe is failing with 503). 

The readiness probe checks `/health?ready=1` which returns 503 because CockroachDB isn't initialized yet. The cluster needs to be initialized with `cockroach init` but it won't be ready until all nodes are up, and all nodes won't be up until pod-0 is ready.

This is the classic chicken-and-egg problem with CockroachDB StatefulSets. 

The fix is to change the readiness probe to just check `/health` without the `?ready=1` parameter, so pods become Ready before cluster initialization. Then I can run `cockroach init` separately as a Job or through kubectl exec once the pods are up. The key insight is that the readiness probe should check `/health` instead of `/health?ready=1` — this way pods become Ready as soon as they're running, before the cluster is initialized. Once all three pods are Ready, I can run `cockroach init` to complete the setup.

Updating the StatefulSet with this corrected readiness probe should resolve the deadlock.

content.text: Pod-0 is failing its readiness probe because the cluster isn't initialized yet — CockroachDB returns 503 on `/health?ready=1` before `cockroach init` is run. The StatefulSet blocks pod-1 creation until pod-0 is Ready. I'll fix the readiness probe to use `/health` (no `?ready=1`), let all pods come up, then run `cockroach init`.

input.description: Fix readiness probe to use /health instead of /health?ready=1
Delete pod-0 so StatefulSet recreates it with the updated probe

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods --no-headers 2>/dev/null)
  READY=$(echo "$STATUS" | grep -c "1/1" || true)
  TOTAL=$(echo "$STATUS" | grep -c "crdb-cluster" || true)
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) Ready: $READY/$TOTAL"
  echo "$STATUS"
  if [ "$READY" -ge 3 ]; then echo "All 3 pods Ready"; break; fi
  sleep 5
done
input.description: Poll until all 3 pods are 1/1 Ready
